# Commercial Cleaning Industry Snapshot — TheDealSheet

**Author:** TheDealSheet Editorial
**Source:** [thedealsheet.co](https://thedealsheet.co/industries/commercial-cleaning/)
**Saved:** 2026-04-20

## Why I Saved This

Kay flagged 2026-04-20 that Commercial Cleaning is the next DealsX wave niche if G&B has not closed a deal by ~2026-07-20. This intel is queued for that activation trigger — see [[inbox/2026-07-20-dealsx-next-wave-commercial-cleaning-trigger]]. Captured now so the activation workflow has industry data ready without re-fetching.

## Content

### Market Size & Growth
- U.S. market: $112B (2026)
- CAGR: 5.6% (2025–2033) per TheDealSheet; 4.2–4.8% per IBISWorld
- Revenue mix: Commercial 77% / Residential 17% / Damage & Specialty 6%

### Fragmentation
Top 50 companies = only ~30% of revenue. 351K annual job openings signal thousands of operators.

### Margins (G&B buy-box: $2–10M revenue)
| Metric | Industry | Top quartile |
|---|---|---|
| Gross margin | 25–35% | 38%+ |
| EBITDA margin | 12–18% | 20%+ |
| SDE margin | ~25% median | — |
| Labor % revenue | 50–60% | <48% |

Service gross margins: office 18–25% / healthcare 22–30% / carpet 30–40% / floor strip-wax 28–38% / green 25–35% / post-construction 20–28%.

### Valuation Multiples
| Band | Multiple | Metric |
|---|---|---|
| $500K–$1.5M | 2.0–2.6x | SDE |
| $1.5M–$3M | 2.3–2.9x | SDE |
| $3M–$5M | 2.5–3.1x | SDE |
| $5M+ platform | 3.4–4.5x | EBITDA |

Premium drivers: 40%+ recurring w/ 75%+ renewal, no client >15% revenue, tech stack, specialty services, green certs, <50% turnover.

### Recurring Revenue
- Floor: 40%+ recurring commercial = valuation threshold
- Premium: 60%+ recurring, 75%+ renewal
- Retention: 75–85% industry / 90%+ top quartile

### Growth Drivers
Post-COVID hygiene / OSHA, outsourcing acceleration, commercial RE expansion, tech/automation adoption, green cleaning (10–20% price premium), essential non-discretionary profile.

### PE Activity
M&A pace: 2022 ~100 → 2023 ~100 → 2024 138 (+32% YoY) → 2025 H1 95. 2025: 90 PE-backed deals, 80%+ bolt-ons.

Named platforms:
- 4M Building Services (O2 Investment Partners)
- Kleen-Tech Services (Rainier Partners) — 9 brands, 30+ states
- KBS / Kellermeyer Bergensons (Cerberus Capital)
- Kept Companies (DFW Capital / ACON Investments) — 120+ tuck-ins, 23 platforms
- Solid Surface Care (Angeles Equity Partners)

### Operational Challenges
- 351K unfilled openings, 75–200% turnover, <50% premium
- Wages 4%/yr faster than revenue; 25% revenue growth vs 17% earnings (margin compression)
- 63% cite staffing as primary risk (BSCAI 2026)
- Compliance $5–15K/yr rising (OSHA, EPA, TSCA PFAS, EU DPP 2026)

### 2026 Regulatory Changes
CA Prop 65 amendments (Q1 2025); TSCA PFAS reporting (Apr 2026); EPA Safer Choice VOC/microplastic (Q2 2025); EPA PBT phase-out (Oct 2026); EU Digital Product Passport (Jul 2026).

### Competitive Landscape
No national competitors named (fragmented). Threats: low barriers, tech-enabled operators, residential commoditization (0.5–1.0x discount), labor scarcity.

## Key Patterns

- Pattern 1: Fragmented + recurring + PE-backed validation matches G&B thesis architecture (same as Premium Pest Management)
- Pattern 2: **Margins are lower than G&B's premium-services thesis assumes** — 12–18% EBITDA industry vs. 25–35%+ at Premium Pest. Requires tech-enabled or specialty sub-niche positioning (healthcare/biotech/data-center cleaning commands 10–30% premium + higher margins)
- Pattern 3: Labor crisis is the dominant operational risk — operator selection must weight retention systems heavily
- Pattern 4: Owner-age data missing from source — niche-intelligence must verify on activation before committing to succession thesis
- Pattern 5: "Specialty within cleaning" (healthcare/biotech/data centers) may be the G&B-shaped sub-niche, analogous to how Premium Pest = commercial-luxury sub-niche of general pest

## How I Might Use This

- On 2026-07-20 activation trigger: feed this into niche-intelligence Step 1 (one-pager seed) + scorecard
- Compare margin profile vs G&B scorecard floors — if 12–18% EBITDA fails scorecard, sub-niche to specialty cleaning (healthcare/biotech) rather than discarding the niche
- Use the 5 named PE platforms as competitive screen during list-building — exclude portfolio companies
- Direct port of Premium Pest one-pager template structure (fragmented + recurring + PE-validated + compliance tailwind)
